site.name: American Eagle Australia
site.mechanism_justification: Localised Inc. platform (localised-1.66) is a pure client-side SPA with no server-side rendering. The Product REST API (/api/v2/products/{code}) returns 401 without a session token. The SPA renders everything client-side via React, so Playwright is required to render the page and extract product data. Additionally, the Algolia Search API credentials are embedded in the metadata (account: 1ZEQOKJJX6, key: a7166f47083cb5ba718669c3e9a348a9) and can be used via browser fetch() to look up products by code in the prd-product_AU Algolia index as a faster alternative.

anti_bot.details: No Cloudflare, Akamai, or PerimeterX detected. CAPTCHA is configured but only for paymentInitiate and storeCard actions (not browsing). The probe_page succeeded with Playwright (no proxy) with HTTP 200. No bot detection challenges observed.

connectivity.notes: Direct HTTP returns empty SPA shell (no SSR). Playwright with no proxy successfully loads the page. No proxy escalation needed. The site is a Localised Inc. React SPA that loads all content client-side.

product_discovery.url_pattern_notes: Product URLs follow the pattern: /en-au/product/{slug}/{style_id}_{color_id}_{sku_id}?color={color_name}. The critical identifier is the product code (e.g., 1165_5323_131). The slug is human-readable. Color query parameter is optional but sets the selected variant. The product code can be extracted from URLs and used to look up products via the Algolia Search API (index: prd-product_AU).

scraping_strategy.description: Use Playwright to navigate to each product URL, wait for the SPA to render, then extract product data from the DOM. Monitor network requests to capture API responses from /api/v2/products/ which contain structured product data.

extraction_methods.description: Intercept XHR/fetch requests to /api/v2/products/{code} during page load. The SPA makes this call after session creation and it returns structured JSON with all product data (price, variants, images, descriptions, availability).

Use browser fetch() with Algolia credentials to search the prd-product_AU index using the product code (objectID). This bypasses session creation entirely and returns structured product data directly.
Extract product data from the rendered DOM using CSS selectors after the SPA has fully loaded. Fall back to this if network interception and Algolia both fail.

rate_limiting.justification: No anti-bot protection detected, but being respectful to avoid rate limits. Localised platform behind CloudFront CDN. Algolia has generous rate limits for search operations.
